question_text: Two identical containers are filled with different liquids. Container A has liquid with a density of 0.8 g/cm³ and Container B has liquid with a density of 1.2 g/cm³. A steel ball with a density of 7.8 g/cm³ is placed in each container. What will happen to the steel ball in each container?
correct_answer: The steel ball will sink in both containers. In Container A, the ball (7.8 g/cm³) is denser than the liquid (0.8 g/cm³), so it sinks. In Container B, the ball (7.8 g/cm³) is also denser than the liquid (1.2 g/cm³), so it sinks in both cases.
explanation: An object sinks when its density is greater than the density of the liquid it's placed in. Since the steel ball (7.8 g/cm³) is much denser than both liquids, it will sink in both containers. The density of the liquid doesn't need to be exactly the same as the object—only the comparison matters.
